input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `$(IsTestProject)` property, which `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets to `true` automatically. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- shared props for all projects … -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** any project that references the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package gets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Library/production projects never reference that package, so the condition is `false` and they stay as libraries.

**Tip:** if the timing matters (the property is set by an SDK-style import that runs after `Directory.Build.props`), move the block to `Directory.Build.targets` instead — it evaluates after all package props are imported, so `$(IsTestProject)` is guaranteed to be available.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditions. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references the test framework package (like `MSTest.TestAdapter` or `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) or to use a naming convention.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on `$(IsTestProject)`, which is set automatically by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, or to use a condition based on the project name convention.


Now I'll provide a practical solution with the specific MSBuild conditions needed.
